steps.title: Identify the key constraint in the sediment evidence
steps.content: The strongest signal is that fine mudstone—the predominant sediment type—shows episodic desiccation cracks. This reveals two requirements: a persistent water environment (to deposit mudstone) that periodically dried, plus the coarse sandstone intervals indicate flowing water at times.

steps.title: Match to the choices
steps.content:
a lake existed at the Murray Formation for a prolonged period, though the lake occasionally experienced drying and there were periods in which one or more streams were present. directly proposes this: a prolonged lake that occasionally dried with intermittent streams.

It's the only choice that explains all three features of the evidence in a coherent sequence.
  • B reverses the temporal order
  • C contradicts the desiccation data
  • D eliminates the lake


That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
